original_hypothesis.title: RouterV2.swapExactTokensForTokensSimple Sends Tokens to address(0) Due to Uninitialized Route Fields
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: swapExactTokensForTokensSimple creates a route struct in memory but never initializes the `pair` or `receiver` fields, leaving them as address(0). getAmountsOut uses routes[i].pair to compute amounts: for non-concentrated, isPair(address(0)) returns false so amounts[1] stays 0 causing revert on amountOutMin check. For concentrated, pairToDeployer(address(0)) returns address(0) causing quoter failure. Even if bypassed, _swap sends output to routes[0].receiver = address(0), permanently losing tokens.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a core user-facing swap function that is completely non-functional. Any user calling this function will waste gas on a guaranteed revert. If amountOutMin is 0 and concentrated=true finds a path, tokens would be sent to address(0).

proof_of_signal.pattern: route[] memory routes = new route[](1); sets from/to/stable/concentrated but never sets routes[0].pair or routes[0].receiver; defaults to address(0)

preconditions: User calls swapExactTokensForTokensSimple with any parameters
No special configuration needed - always fails

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Uninitialized struct fields in memory default to zero values
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: routes[0].pair = address(0) breaks getAmountsOut; routes[0].receiver = address(0) would send tokens to burn address
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No assignment of pair = pairFor(...) or receiver = to

analysis_questions: Is this function used by any frontend or integration?
Can concentrated=true path bypass the revert and actually send tokens to address(0)?
Is there any way getAmountsOut succeeds with pair=address(0)?
validation_requirements: Test both concentrated and non-concentrated paths

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: User calls swapExactTokensForTokensSimple to swap 1000 USDC for WAVAX. Function reverts on getAmountsOut, wasting gas. If somehow bypassed, tokens go to address(0).
prerequisites: Any user interaction with this function

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Gas loss on every call; potential permanent token loss if revert is bypassed
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users attempting to use this swap function
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Gas fees unrecoverable; tokens at address(0) unrecoverable

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observable as consistent revert from this function

validation_result.mechanism: uninitialized struct fields in memory route
validation_result.impact_summary: swapExactTokensForTokensSimple is completely non-functional. routes[0].pair and routes[0].receiver default to address(0), causing getAmountsOut to return 0 (non-concentrated) or revert (concentrated), and _swap to target address(0) as receiver.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The swapExactTokensForTokensSimple function at RouterV2.sol lines 293-314 creates a route[] memory array of length 1 and sets .from, .to, .stable, and .concentrated, but never sets .pair or .receiver. In Solidity, memory-allocated struct fields default to their zero values, so routes[0].pair = address(0) and routes[0].receiver = address(0).

For the non-concentrated path:
1. getAmountsOut checks IBaseV1Factory(factory).isPair(routes[i].pair) where routes[i].pair = address(0). isPair(address(0)) returns false since address(0) is never registered as a pair.
2. Since the isPair check fails, amounts[1] is never assigned and stays at 0.
3. If amountOutMin > 0, the function reverts at require(amounts[amounts.length - 1] >= amountOutMin, 'IOA').
4. If amountOutMin = 0, the require passes, tokens are transferred to the real pair via pairFor(), but _swap tries pair.swap(0, 0, address(0), ...) which reverts in Pair.swap with 'IOA' (INSUFFICIENT_OUTPUT_AMOUNT).

For the concentrated path:
1. getAmountsOut calls pairToDeployer(address(0)) which returns address(0), then passes deployer=address(0) to the quoter, which will fail.
2. Even if a non-zero amount were returned, _swap sets recipient = routes[i].receiver = address(0), permanently burning output tokens.

The _to parameter passed to _swap is completely dead code - _swap never uses it, only routes[i].receiver.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is accurate. Lines 293-314 of RouterV2.sol show the route construction that omits pair and receiver. The route struct definition at lines 83-90 confirms these are required fields. The getAmountsOut function at lines 218-248 uses routes[i].pair. The _swap function at lines 255-287 uses routes[i].receiver. Both fields being address(0) causes the described failures.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: routes[0].pair and routes[0].receiver are never set, defaulting to address(0). getAmountsOut uses routes[i].pair for price quoting, and _swap uses routes[i].receiver as the output destination. Both being address(0) breaks the function entirely.
When routes[i].pair is address(0), isPair(address(0)) returns false so amounts[i+1] stays 0. For concentrated, pairToDeployer(address(0)) returns address(0) causing quoter failure.

_swap ignores the _to parameter entirely and uses routes[i].receiver for the swap destination. Since receiver is address(0), tokens would be sent to the zero address if the swap somehow executed. The _to parameter is dead code.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls swapExactTokensForTokensSimple with valid token parameters
2. route[] memory routes = new route[](1) creates route with pair=address(0), receiver=address(0)
3. routes[0].from/to/stable/concentrated are set, but NOT routes[0].pair or routes[0].receiver
5a. Non-concentrated: isPair(address(0)) returns false → amounts[1] stays 0 → reverts at amountOutMin check (or continues to _swap with amount=0)
5b. Concentrated: pairToDeployer(address(0)) returns address(0) → quoter fails/reverts
6. If somehow reaching _swap: pair.swap(0, 0, address(0), ...) reverts with 'IOA' in Pair.swap
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: swapExactTokensForTokensSimple creates the route at line 303-307 without setting pair or receiver. getAmountsOut at line 238 checks isPair(routes[i].pair) where pair=address(0), returning false. amounts[1] stays 0. If amountOutMin=0 passes the check at line 308, _swap at line 274 attempts pair.swap(0, 0, address(0), ...) which reverts in Pair.sol line 322 with 'IOA' since both amount0Out and amount1Out are 0.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: The deployment script V1/DeployRouterV2.js deploys RouterV2 with factory and wETH parameters, but the bug is in the function logic itself, not deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Any user calling swapExactTokensForTokensSimple will experience a guaranteed revert, wasting gas. The function is completely non-functional regardless of parameters. For non-concentrated: isPair(address(0)) → false → amounts[1]=0 → revert. For concentrated: pairToDeployer(address(0)) → address(0) → quoter failure → revert. There is no parameter combination that allows successful execution.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity code defect. A core user-facing swap function (swapExactTokensForTokensSimple) is completely broken and will always revert. Any frontend or integration calling this function wastes user gas. The function accepts 8 parameters and appears to be a convenience wrapper, but is entirely non-functional due to missing pair and receiver initialization. This is compounded by the fact that _swap ignores the _to parameter (dead code), so even fixing the pair field alone would not fix the receiver issue.
recommended_mitigations: Set routes[0].pair = pairFor(tokenFrom, tokenTo, stable) for non-concentrated routes, or the appropriate Algebra pool address for concentrated routes
Set routes[0].receiver = to for the last hop (single-hop in this case)
Consider refactoring _swap to use the _to parameter for the final hop receiver instead of relying on routes[i].receiver, matching typical router patterns
Add integration tests for swapExactTokensForTokensSimple covering both concentrated and non-concentrated paths
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy PairFactory, PairGenerator, and create a token pair. 2. Deploy RouterV2 with factory and WAVAX. 3. Call swapExactTokensForTokensSimple with valid tokens, stable=false, concentrated=false, amountOutMin=0. 4. Observe revert. 5. Also test with concentrated=true to confirm quoter failure path.
uncertainties: The exact revert reason for concentrated path depends on external Algebra quoter behavior when deployer=address(0), which is out of scope
It's possible the protocol team intends this function to be deprecated in favor of swapExactTokensForTokens with user-provided routes, but the function remains public and callable
additional_testing_needed: Verify no frontend or integration currently calls swapExactTokensForTokensSimple
Test swapExactTokensForTokens with user-provided routes to confirm it works when pair and receiver are properly set
Verify that BlackholePairAPIV2.getAmountOut correctly constructs routes with pair and receiver fields for the frontend
